story.iWant: the EndorInitializer to wire dependencies automatically
story.soThat: I can configure the entire service graph in one place
story.tasks: - [ ] Task 1: Design EndorInitializer dependency injection architecture (AC: 1) - [ ] Task 2: Implement custom dependency registration methods (AC: 2, 5) - [ ] Task 3: Add comprehensive dependency graph validation (AC: 3) - [ ] Task 4: Ensure backward compatibility with default implementations (AC: 4) - [ ] Task 5: Implement resource management and lifecycle handling (AC: 6) - [ ] Task 6: Comprehensive testing and integration validation (AC: 1-6)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. **Dependency Graph Configuration**: EndorInitializer.Build() creates properly wired service instances using the dependency injection container established in Story 2.1 2. **Custom Dependency Registration**: Initializer provides hooks for custom dependency registration through WithCustomRepository(), WithCustomConfig(), and WithContainer() methods 3. **Complete Dependency Validation**: Initializer validates the complete dependency graph before starting and provides clear error messages for dependency configuration problems 4. **Backward Compatibility**: Simple use cases continue to work without modification, using default dependency implementations from previous stories 5. **Advanced Customization**: Advanced users can override any dependency with custom implementations through explicit configuration methods 6. **Proper Resource Management**: Framework ensures proper dependency cleanup during shutdown following dependency order (dependents before dependencies)

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: FR12: Framework supports both constructor injection and factory patterns. FR21: Framework provides clear error messages for dependency injection failures

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Lightweight Custom DI Container with type-safe registration, lifecycle management, and validation

doc.title: Epic 2: Dependency Injection Architecture
doc.snippet: EndorInitializer.Build() creates properly wired service instances with dependency graph validation

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Enhanced EndorInitializer to handle automatic dependency wiring with container integration

artifact.reason: Current EndorInitializer that needs enhancement with DI container integration
Container-based service creation pattern to integrate with EndorInitializer
Hybrid service container integration for EndorInitializer.Build()
Repository factory patterns established in Story 2.4 for automatic dependency injection
DI container implementation from Story 2.1 for dependency resolution
Default dependency providers for backward compatibility
Current initialization pattern showing EndorInitializer usage

story-context.constraints: - EndorInitializer.Build() MUST validate complete dependency graph before service creation - All dependency resolution MUST occur through DI container established in Story 2.1 - Custom dependency registration MUST override defaults while maintaining interface compliance - Error messages MUST provide clear guidance for fixing dependency configuration issues - Backward compatibility MUST be preserved for existing EndorInitializer usage patterns - Dependency cleanup MUST follow proper order (dependents before dependencies) - Integration with repository factory patterns from Story 2.4 is mandatory - Service creation must use NewEndorServiceFromContainer() and NewEndorHybridServiceFromContainer()

interface.name: di.Container
interface.signature: Register[T any](impl T, scope Scope), Resolve[T any]() (T, error)

tests.standards: Framework uses testify/mock for behavior verification, separates unit tests (//go:build unit) from integration tests (//go:build integration), and follows repository pattern testing established in Stories 2.1-2.4

ideas.test: Test EndorInitializer.Build() creates properly wired service instances using DI container
Test WithContainer(), WithCustomRepository(), WithCustomConfig() methods provide dependency override
Test dependency graph validation with clear error messages for missing dependencies
Test simple use cases work without modification using default implementations
Test custom implementations can override any dependency through configuration methods
Test proper dependency cleanup during shutdown in dependency order
